senses.definition: to stop or overcome a behavior or practice that has become a regular part of one's life
to intentionally interrupt a moment of silence with a comment or action, usually to keep the conversation flowing or maintain the audience's attention

examples.x: During the speech, the speaker skillfully broke a pause by sharing a relevant and engaging story.
In a job interview, it's important to break a pause with thoughtful questions to show your interest.

senses.definition: to fail to fulfill a commitment or assurance given to someone

examples.x: Breaking a promise can erode trust in a relationship, so it's essential to communicate openly.

senses.definition: to set a new highest achievement or performance, typically in a competitive context

examples.x: The athlete broke a record at the championship last year.
The world watched in amazement as the daredevil attempted to break a record by skydiving from the highest altitude.

senses.definition: to reduce the impact or prevent injury when landing after a fall or sudden drop

examples.x: The gymnast expertly twisted her body to break her fall and avoid any serious injury.
Falling from the ladder, he extended his arm to break his fall and avoid landing directly on his back.
He grabbed onto a branch to break his fall while climbing the tree.
The stuntman uses special padding to break his fall when jumping from great heights in movies.

senses.definition: to stop a planned holiday early and go back to work or home due to unforeseen circumstances

examples.x: They had to break their vacation and return home because of a family emergency.
The unexpected work assignment required him to break his vacation and go back to the office.
Breaking your vacation can be disappointing, but sometimes it's necessary to address urgent matters.
While on their road trip, they received news that forced them to break their vacation and head back immediately.

senses.definition: to temporarily stop traveling during a trip, often for rest or to explore a place, before continuing

examples.x: He needed to break a journey that was becoming monotonous and tiresome.

senses.definition: to inform someone of important or disturbing information

examples.x: She had to break the news gently to her friend that their planned trip had to be postponed.
It's never easy to break the news of a loved one's passing to family members.
Breaking the news of a company's bankruptcy to its employees was a challenging task for the CEO.
Parents often have to find the right way to break the news to their children about major life changes.
The journalist was the first to break the news of the groundbreaking scientific discovery.

senses.definition: to end a period of quietness or lack of communication by speaking, making noise, or taking action

examples.x: In the serene forest, the chirping birds were the first to break the silence of the morning.
The tense meeting needed someone to break the silence and start the discussion.
After a minute of contemplation, he decided to break the silence by sharing his thoughts.
She couldn't bear the awkwardness any longer and decided to break the silence with a lighthearted comment.
Sometimes, a simple gesture can break the silence and convey more than words ever could.

senses.definition: to end a magical or enchanting influence or to free someone from its effects

examples.x: The ancient sorcerer broke the spell with a rare and ancient artifact.
As we speak, the wizard is breaking the spell that has plagued the village for centuries.
The villagers worked together to break a spell that had engulfed their town for generations, bringing them freedom at last.
The intense heat caused everyone in the room to break out in a sweat despite the air conditioning.
After running a marathon, Jane broke out in a sweat as she crossed the finish line.
Waiting for the delayed train on the crowded platform, passengers started to break out in a sweat.

word_list.pronunciation: bɹˈeɪk ˈaʊt ɪn ɐ kˈoʊld swˈɛt

senses.definition: to get very nervous or scared

examples.x: Hearing the unexpected noise in the dark alley, he broke out in a cold sweat, fearing the unknown.
When she realized she had forgotten her presentation notes, she broke out in a cold sweat before the important meeting.
The haunted house was so scary that visitors would often break out in a cold sweat as they walked through it.

senses.definition: to reach a point where gains equal losses, resulting in a balance

examples.x: The startup is projected to break even after a few more months of operation.
The company struggled to break even in the first year of operation.
Despite challenges, the project managed to break even by careful financial planning.
The restaurant aimed to break even after a slow month of sales.
